name: South Ostrobothnia (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the historic Ostrobothnian arms, evoking the lakes and skies of the region
the silver heraldic charges of the arms

description: A square blue flag charged with the silver provincial arms of Ostrobothnia: a crowned arm holding an arrow above six ermine spots arranged in three rows.
history: South Ostrobothnia (Etelä-Pohjanmaa) is one of the regions formed in Finland's 1997 administrative reform. Its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic arms of the broader Ostrobothnia province.

features.symbolism: the historic provincial arms of Ostrobothnia: six silver ermine spots arranged 3-2-1, with a crowned arm grasping an arrow above. The arms refer to the medieval ermine-fur trade that gave Ostrobothnia its early prosperity.
